body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The author requests a `CompleteMessagesAsync` overload on `ServiceBusMessageActions` that accepts a collection of messages for bulk completion, analogous to the existing batch API on `ServiceBusReceiver`, to improve efficiency in high-throughput Azure Functions scenarios with `AutoCompleteMessages = false`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.ServiceBus`
**Affected API:** `ServiceBusMessageActions.CompleteMessageAsync` (single-message overload only)
**Scenarios:**
- Azure Function App using Service Bus trigger with `AutoCompleteMessages = false` processing batches of messages
- High-frequency applications that need to complete many messages in a single round-trip

**Root ask:** Add a `CompleteMessagesAsync(IEnumerable<ServiceBusReceivedMessage>)` overload to `ServiceBusMessageActions` mirroring the batch completion API available on `ServiceBusReceiver`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a feature request, not a bug — no crash or incorrect behavior is reported.

**Investigation notes:**
- The standard `ServiceBusReceiver.CompleteMessagesAsync` already supports batch completion; parity with the trigger's `ServiceBusMessageActions` is the ask.
- Consider whether the underlying AMQP settlement calls can be batched server-side or whether this would just be a client-side loop convenience wrapper.
- Check if settlement disposition batching is supported by the Service Bus AMQP protocol/broker to understand the real efficiency gain.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `ServiceBusMessageActions` in `sdk/servicebus/Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.ServiceBus` for existing settlement methods.
2. Compare with `ServiceBusReceiver.CompleteMessagesAsync` to understand how batch completion is implemented there.
3. Determine whether a new overload should be added to `ServiceBusMessageActions` or whether documentation/guidance pointing users to an alternative pattern would suffice.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The request is about SDK API surface (`ServiceBusMessageActions`) behavior, not a REST/service-level issue.
- **Service:** `Service Bus` — The named package is `Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.ServiceBus`, which lives under `/sdk/servicebus/`. Reference issues #41451 and #39073 confirm the "Service Bus" + "Client" pattern for this area.
- **Confidence:** High — Package is explicitly named, service directory is unambiguous, category is clearly Client.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Service Bus` (line 420) — matched because predicted label "Service Bus" is present.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @jsquire
- **ServiceOwners:** @EldertGrootenboer @j7nw4r @skarri-microsoft
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@jsquire` (single AzureSdkOwner); added `needs-team-attention` (issue is customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; no multi-label entry for `%Service Bus` with another label was encountered before the `%Service Bus` catch-all at line 420, so this entry matched first.
</details>
